summary: Jest test assertion failure in test/config/get.ts. The test expects `pnpm config get` to return a registry URL without a trailing slash, but the actual output includes a trailing slash.
root_cause: The test assertion at test/config/get.ts:32 expects the registry URL 'https://my-org.registry.example.com' but receives 'https://my-org.registry.example.com/' with a trailing slash appended.
relevant_tests: pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

evidence.quote:
FAIL test/config/get.ts (17.807 s)
  ● pnpm config get reads npm options but ignores other settings from .npmrc

    expect(received).toBe(expected) // Object.is equality

    Expected: "https://my-org.registry.example.com"
    Received: "https://my-org.registry.example.com/"
evidence.reason: Explicit test failure showing a string mismatch; the received value has a trailing slash that is not in the expected value.
The failing assertion code and line number confirm the exact point of failure in the test file.

evidence.quote:
Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
Tests:       1 failed, 9 skipped, 475 passed, 485 total
evidence.reason: Only one test failed out of 485; this is an isolated assertion failure, not a widespread test suite problem.

suggested_fix: Update the test expectation in test/config/get.ts:32 to expect the trailing slash: `.toBe('https://my-org.registry.example.com/')`, or verify that a recent code change introduced trailing slash normalization in registry URL handling and adjust the test or code accordingly.
